pi_items.source: task.json line 17, confirmed by setup_task.sh line 9, export_result.sh line 6, and verifier.py line 75
task.json line 18 claims 50 KB, but verifier.py line 48 checks: 'if result.get("file_size_bytes", 0) > 10240:' which is 10 KB, not 50 KB

task.json line 19, setup_task.sh line 8 specifies '/home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium', setup_invesalius.sh line 70-73 confirms this is a symlink to the 0051 DICOM dataset from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip
setup_invesalius.sh line 73, verified through curl HEAD request (HTTP 302 redirect to release asset), file size 24.3 MB (25,165,824 bytes)
DICOM file inspection using pydicom of /tmp/dicom_extract/0051/1.3.12.2.1107.5.1.1.20706.20020604105245122.4.dcm shows Modality: CT

pi_items.metadata_value: skull/cranial region (implied by task name and description)
pi_items.source: DICOM file inspection shows BodyPartExamined='BASE 3MM' (skull base region), PatientName='CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample'
Zip archive listing of 0051.zip shows 109 files total (108 .dcm files plus 1 directory)
DICOM file inspection shows PatientName: 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample'
DICOM file inspection shows Rows: 512, Columns: 512
DICOM file inspection shows DICOM files dated 2002-06-04 in the archive

privileged_info_summary: The CT Cranium (0051) is a real, publicly available medical dataset from InVesalius 3's official GitHub releases (v3.0), consisting of 109 DICOM files of a human skull/cranial region acquired on 2002-06-04. The dataset is available at https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip (24.3 MB). The task requires exporting a screenshot of a 3D sagittal cross-section to /home/ga/Documents/skull_cross_section.png. CRITICAL FINDING: The task metadata claims min_file_size_kb: 50, but the verifier implementation only enforces > 10 KB (10240 bytes). This is a contradiction between the metadata specification and the actual verification logic.
